name: bounded-resource-pool
description: A standard-library-only, generic semaphore-based bounded resource pool. Package "pool" provides two types: Semaphore, a counting semaphore built on the classic chan-struct{}-as-semaphore idiom (blocking Acquire, non-blocking TryAcquire, Release, Len, Cap, panics on non-positive capacity), and Pool[T any], a generic fixed-capacity resource pool backed by a buffered channel that doubles as the semaphore (Acquire blocks until a resource frees, TryAcquire is the non-blocking variant, Release returns a resource, Available/Cap report state, New returns ErrEmptyPool when seeded with no resources). Table-driven tests cover empty-pool validation, TryAcquire exhaustion and recovery, acquire/release round-trips, blocking-until-release semantics, semaphore capacity bounds, panic-on-bad-capacity, and a concurrency invariant test that asserts holders never exceed capacity. Verified green with gofmt -l, go build ./..., go vet ./..., go test ./..., and go test -race ./... using go1.25.3 against the go 1.23 module.

maintain.request: Add an AcquireContext(ctx context.Context) (T, error) method to Pool so callers can perform a context-bounded blocking checkout: it returns a resource when one becomes free, or the zero value and ctx.Err() if the context is cancelled or its deadline expires first (so a slot is never consumed on failure).

review.bug: New allocates the resource channel with capacity len(items)-1, so pre-filling the pool overflows the buffer and the final send blocks forever, deadlocking construction (an off-by-one buffer size).
